description_long: This is a two-story suburban house with a prominent dark shingle gable roof and a brick exterior. The front facade features a covered entryway with a small overhang, flanked by large rectangular windows and a white front door. On one side, there is an attached garage with a black door, and the windows are symmetrically arranged on both floors. The roof has a textured, overlapping shingle pattern, and the house includes both standard and bay windows for added architectural interest. The rear and side views reveal additional windows and a secondary door, with the brickwork continuing around the structure. The overall design is practical and typical of North American residential neighborhoods.
description: A two-story brick house with a dark gable shingle roof, large windows, and an attached garage. The front features a covered entryway and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_0: Front and side of the house with a covered entryway, white door, and large windows; garage partially visible.
description_view_1: Rear and roof view showing the shingle texture, brick walls, and minimal window detail.
description_view_2: Full front view with the main entryway, bay windows, and the attached garage clearly visible.
description_view_3: Back and side view highlighting the roof's overhang and the absence of major entry points.
attribute_entryway_design: Covered entryway with a small overhang, white door, and adjacent bay windows
